observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
Designate another qualified Disbursing Officer if additional cash advances are necessary and justified under the circumstances; or
Increase the amount of fidelity bond of the Disbursing Officer to sufficiently cover her cash accountabilities.

We recommended that the Collecting Officers prepare the RCDs at the close of each business day and to remit their collections on a daily basis.  Further, we directed all Collecting Officers ensure that all particulars in the Official receipts were completely filled out without any erasures or alterations.

We recommended that management exert more effort to collect these real property tax delinquencies that have been long overdue in order for the municipality to augment its income that could be used for other projects that would be beneficial to the constituents of the municipality.

We recommended that the Inventory Committee and Municipal Accountant complete the necessary procedures and reports to establish the correct balances of the PPE items that shall be reported in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Inventory Committee prepare a Report of Physical Count of Local Road Network (RPCLRN) using the prescribed format.
Additionally, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain a subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network and prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of depreciation for each component at the end of the year.
Furthermore, the General Services Office maintain a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all the roads and their components.
Finally, ensure that the required disclosures are included in the notes to the financial statements.

We recommended that the General Service Officer, together with the Disposal Committee, finalize the necessary documents/forms and dispose the unserviceable properties in accordance with COA-DBM-Joint Circular 2024-01.
Further, upon disposal, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant drop the cost of the unserviceable/obsolete PPEs from the books to reflect the accurate value of PPE in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Agriculture’s Office and the Accounting Office conduct a reconciliation the Breeding Stocks account and that adjustments be effected in either or both records to arrive at reliable balances of accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant re-evaluate each payable accounts to ascertain the existence of actual claims so that the required reversion of items, which had been in the books for two years or more, would be effected. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant send confirmations to the claimants prior to the reversion of the long outstanding payables.

We recommended that management conduct a thorough operation review of the Waterworks and Market operations to address the root causes and implement corrective actions aimed at improving operations and preventing ongoing financial losses.

We recommended that management submit an explanation for the inconsistencies between the data in the Extension of Time Nos. 1 to 4 and their corresponding Technical Reports as well as the granting of Extension of Time No. 4 despite absence of supporting documents.  Additionally, submit the lacking supporting documents noted in the audit and any other documents to support the evaluation of the extensions made.

We recommended that management fully institutionalize the LDRRMO by creating and filling up the statutory/mandatory positions required under the NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) 2014-1 to assure the implementation of actions and measures related to disaster risk reduction and management.

We recommended that management immediately submit all lacking documents to the audit team. Furthermore, we recommended that the Municipal Account shall only process disbursement vouchers accompanied with complete supporting documents pursuant to Section 4(6) of PD 1445.

We recommended that the municipality review the composition of officers and members of AGEMCO and avoid any possible conflict of interest that may arise in their procurement activities with the said cooperative.

Management ensures to trace and locate relevant documents to serve as a basis for analyzing prior years’ reconciling items and preparing necessary adjustments. Moreover, require the Municipal Treasurer to settle these accounts to allow the creditor-fund to utilize the amount for its intended purpose.

Petty Cash Custodian uses the prescribed Petty Cash Voucher when payment is made out of the Petty Cash Fund as required under Paragraph 3, Section 48 of the New Government Accounting System Manual for Local Government Units.

The Municipal Treasurer prepares and submits regularly to the Office of the Auditor the Monthly Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms With and Without Money Value to ensure compliance with the afore-cited regulation, and to monitor its use and condition. We further recommend upgrading of its ETRACS to facilitate the generation of RAAF and other enhancements useful to the agency.

We recommended and management agreed that the municipal officials involved in the processing of Confidential Funds comply with the provisions of COA-DBM-DILG-DND-Governance Commission for GOCCs JMC No. 2015-01, particularly in the release and liquidation thereof in order to facilitate timely review of the Cash Advance and immediately communicate to management the deficiencies noted, if any.

The BAC submits a BAC Resolution recommending negotiated procurement under emergency cases duly approved by the HoPE to purchase of one set of Submersible Pump. 
Also, BAC submits justification to support a negotiated procurement under emergency cases on buying the other one (1) set submersible pump. We also recommended that the BAC, henceforth, ensure compliance on instances when negotiated procurement under emergency cases may be resorted, following Annex H of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184.

The management discontinues granting advance payments/mobilization fees unless such requests are duly supported with an irrevocable standby letter of credit, bank guarantee or a surety bond as provided in Section 4.2, Annex E of IRR of RA 9184.

Municipal Accountant sees to it that claims for repairs are supported with Pre-repair/Post-repair Inspection Reports, Waste Material Reports, and records on repair history as required under Section 9.1.3.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-001. 
    
The General Services Office also reconstruct the maintenance/repair record of each vehicle owned and operated by the municipal government.

Management immediately submits all lacking documents to the audit team. 
    
Furthermore, the Municipal Accountant shall processes only disbursement vouchers with complete supporting documents pursuant to Section 4(6) of PD 1445.
The Human Resource Management Officer (HRMO) and Municipal Accountant ensures the proper and complete documentation before payment of wages to the Job Order workers.

The Municipal Accountant check that all POs include brand names and complete detailed specifications of the items procured before processing the payment of the said transactions.  

The Municipal Account sees to it that in the procurement of catering services, the complete detailed list of food items to be served is indicated in the Purchase Orders and Request for Price Quotations.

The BAC Chairman/ Secretariat furnishes the Commission on Audit with copies of the perfected contracts together with the complete supporting documents within five (5) days after approval in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
Management requires the suppliers to issue Official Receipts or Sales Invoices in the procurement of goods and services and adhere to the provision of COA Circular 2023-004 dated June 14, 2023.

The Municipal Mayor instructs the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) and Municipal Engineer to closely coordinate in planning and prioritizing the projects funded under the 20% Development Fund to maximize the utilization of the fund.

Management maximizes the utilization of the 70% component of the LDRRMF to strengthen the municipality’s capacity to build disaster-resilient communities and to enhance disaster preparedness at all levels.
Management incorporates the procedures set forth in COA Circular 2012-002 and NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC 2013-1 upon the release and utilization of the unexpended balances of the LDRRMF.
The LDRRM Officer promptly submits the Monthly Report of Sources and Utilization of DRRMF, duly certified correct by the Municipal Accountant, on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.
The management establishes and maintains a GAD database containing sex-disaggregated data and gender statistics, in compliance with the PCW/NEDA/DBM Joint Circular No. 2012-01.

We recommend the Municipal Engineer/BAC Chairman to justify/explain why the labels of the drawings indicated Design and Build which was not indicated in all the bidding documents for the project Rehabilitation/Improvement of Antequera Waterworks System attaching the documents to support their claim as a Design and Build Project.

	Moreover, please submit the foregoing requirements of the COA-TAS to facilitate inspection of the project.

We recommend the Municipal Engineer/BAC Chairman to submit the lacking documents as mentioned in our above observation if existing and justify/explain the above-noted deficiencies which is the inconsistencies of the supporting documents supporting the procurement of the Design and Build for the above-mentioned project.

We recommend that management revise/update the revenue code pursuant to Section 191 of RA 7160 through revisiting articles and provisions of the municipality’s existing Revenue Code series of 2011 considering present business environment towards the best interest of the public and the government.  We also recommend that enactment of the revised revenue code be made immediately so the potential increase in collections will be achieved to provide additional funds which can be used to finance programs and projects of LGU.

We recommend the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments in the books recognizing the expenses of all foregoing issued semi-expendable properties to present fairly the financial statements of the LGU as of December 31, 2022.  Henceforth, proper use of accounts shall be observed for fair presentation of the LGUs financial statements.

We also recommend the General Services Officer-Designate to review upon delivery the inventories and properties acquired and make the required forms, registries and reports enumerated in Section 4.7 of COA Circular No. 2022-004.  Reports of issuances of inventories shall be furnished to the Municipal Accountant to recognize expenses in the books.  Issuance of ICS to end-users shall also be made for proper control and monitoring of the semi-expendable properties.

We recommend that Management require the recipient barangays to demand the immediate liquidation of the financial assistance received for its proper recording in the books of the LGU and of the barangay.  Henceforth, a clause on the duration/time frame on the utilization of the fund/implementation of the intended project shall be included in the Memorandum of Agreement to aid the implementing agency in the timely implementation of the project and the source agency to demand implementation of the expected output.

We recommended management to impose the penalties and sanctions provided in Section 5C.04m of the Local Revenue Code series of 2011 for late and non-payment of water bills.  We also recommend the Municipal Treasurer to enforce collections of the delinquent accounts to increase the revenue of the waterworks system to at least lessen the losses in its operation.

We recommended that management require the Municipal Treasurer to exert more effort to enforce massive and strategic collections of real property taxes and to impose the remedies provided in the Local Government Code to maximize the collection of real property taxes and diminish, if not reduced delinquent accounts.  Delinquent accounts that remained uncollected after exhausting all the remedies provided in the Code shall be reported to the Sanggunian to assist the Municipal Treasurer in the enforcement of collection of delinquent RPT accounts.

We recommended management has to see to it that all grant of AICS shall be properly and completely documented and the amount shall be limited to the parameters set in the same memorandum circular to avoid suspension and disallowance in audit. Also, submit the lacking documents for the above granted AICS pursuant to the documentary requirements provided in DSWD MC No. 11, series of 2019 to validate the amount of medical assistance provided to eligible individuals, otherwise the above transactions will be suspended/disallowed.

We recommend Management to grant benefits to the physician deployed under the DTTB as provided in Section 2.8 and 2.11 of DBM-DOH JC No. 1, series of 2012. 
In addition, we recommend management to stop the grant of COLA/monthly allowance to DTTB and submit explanation/justification on the grant of such benefits contrary to the afore-cited provisions of DBM-DOH JC No. 1, series of 2012. 
Henceforth, giving of benefits should be within the provisions of the law to avoid disallowance in audit.

We recommend Management to stop the payment of RATA to the non-full-time IOO and grant honorarium at a rate determined in LBC No. 62 dated July 26, 1996.  Henceforth, adhere to the provisions of laws in the granting of compensation benefits to employees to avoid disallowance in audit. 
We also recommend that management demand from the designated IOO of the MHO to refund the RATA received from the period January 2021 to June 2022 less the accumulated value of honorarium supposedly received from the same period.

We recommend the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments in the books capitalizing the cost of major improvements of the AWS and take up its corresponding depreciation expense to present fairly the financial statements of the LGU.

In order to ensure fair presentation and accountability, we recommend the Municipal Accountant to record supplies and materials purchased in bulk in accordance with the existing accounting rules and regulations pursuant to Sections 51 and 121, Volume I of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) for LGUs.

We recommend that management adhere to the provisions prescribed in the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184 pertaining to warranty security, not only in infrastructure projects but also in the procurement of goods, in order to protect not only the interest of the government but also those of the third parties involved in the execution of the government projects.

We further recommend that future procurements of goods be deducted with the 1% retention money or require a special bank guarantee which is equivalent to at least 1% but not to exceed 5% of the total contract price, otherwise, expenses due to the defects of the project or goods within the warranty period be charged to concerned officials approving the payment.
We recommend management to facilitate the titling of all lots recorded in the books to have legal rights and ownership of these lots.

We recommend Management to submit the lacking documents indicated in Annex A of this report, otherwise, the transaction would be suspended in audit. Henceforth, follow the documentary requirements required under GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 dated April 6, 2020.

We recommend that Management initiate action for the recovery of the amount embezzled.  Verify with the Municipal Assessor if the concerned accountable officer has property in his name and exercise lien over his estate in satisfaction of his indebtedness.

We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to record the transaction in accordance with Section 19 of NGAS Manual for LGUs Volume I and to make the necessary adjustments in the books on the above noted deficiencies in the accounting of rent income.  Henceforth, observe the proper accounting and recording of transactions using the prescribed journal entries to present fairly the financial statement of the LGU.

We recommended that Management adhere to the terms and conditions for all contracts entered into by the LGU. Henceforth, management demand from the proprietor of the L300 van the refund of the cost of fuel consumed amounting ₱11,272.57.

We recommended Management to explain on the designation of an employee in concurrent capacity as Waterworks Supervisor which is not a plantilla position in the LGU.  We further recommend that if management found the position to be necessary, said position should be included in the plantilla position to be valid, otherwise, the management should refrain from granting honoraria despite its designation as concurrent capacity.  Moreover, granting of honoraria should be based on the rates provided in LBC No. 62 to avoid disallowance in audit.

We recommend management to require the contractor to refund the amount of ₱1,548,547.42 pertaining to the computed liquidated damages for delayed completion of the project.  Henceforth, all reasons for time suspensions/extensions have to be properly evaluated to determine the validity of the reasons in accordance with the prescribe laws, rules and regulations.  Accordingly impose liquidated damages for all delayed completions of projects.

We recommend management to put in use the portable bottle crusher to address the LGU’s problem on bottle/glass disposal which was the main purpose of its acquisition.  Consequently, issue an ordinance on possible income from sale of the crushed bottles/glasses once the unit is use.

Management to comply with Section 8 of RA 11313 and Section 9 of its IRR to ensure enforcement of the provisions of the Safe Spaces Act. Safety audit shall also be conducted three (3) years after the implementation of this act and every 3 years thereafter pursuant to Section 34 of its IRR.

Use of journal entries as well as preparation of the financial statements shall always be in accordance with the Chart of Accounts as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2015-009, to be able to maintain accurate and reliable accounting records, and to ensure that the financial statements of the municipality are presented fairly.  Consequently, require the adjustment of the above enumerated accounts to its appropriate account titles.

We recommend that future financial assistance for barangay developmental projects be released only once a MOA is made between both parties.  The MOA has to include conditions that in case of failure to implement within a specific period of time, the barangay has to return the amount received.
Further, follow-up has to be made by the concerned municipal officials/department for those barangays with unliquidated financial assistance and compel them to send liquidation reports immediately, otherwise, future request for project funding be denied because of the unliquidated financial assistance.

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to require strict compliance in the recording of liabilities only upon delivery/rendition of goods/services and receipt of bills to prevent the existence of undocumented accounts.

Direct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments in the books of the agency using the above prescribed entries.  Henceforth, observe proper recording and use of accounts to present fairly the financial statements of the municipality.

Direct the Municipal Accountant to review the SWA of the contractors especially if the projects are completed beyond its target date of completion and compute and deduct liquidated damages from the claims of the contractors to avoid disallowance in audit.

We recommend that management evaluate the status of their job order employees and recommend those qualified for permanent positions.  Furthermore, management needs to immediately revisit their vacant plantilla positions and consider the employees with job order status with long years of service for such vacant position.

It is recommended that management submit the lacking/unsubmitted documents to prevent the above transactions from being suspended/disallowed in audit.  Henceforth, the management must adhere to the provisions of Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445 that all claims/payments against the government fund should be completely supported with the necessary documents for the validity of the transactions paid.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant be made to explain on her failure to check the amounts of all the warranty securities posted by the contractor in accordance with the requirements of Section 62.2.3.3 to ensure the sufficiency of the warranty security.  Henceforth, require the contractors to post additional warranty security for insufficient warranties posted for projects that are still within the warranty period.

We recommend that the BAC be required to submit justification for failure to comply the requirement of Section 20 of RIRR of RA No. 9184.  Henceforth, require adherence to the procurement process as outlined in RIRR of RA No. 9184 to avoid suspension/disallowance of transactions.

We recommend that the BAC will verify/examine the bidding documents both during the eligibility check and post-qualification of documents to ascertain that the contract will be awarded to the most qualified bidder.  Adhere to the provisions of RA No. 9184 in the conduct of public bidding to avoid suspension/disallowance in audit.

We recommend that the BAC will check all bidding documents and ensure that these are dated based on the actual occurrence of the procurement process to avoid inconsistences of the dates in the bidding documents.  Adhere to the provisions of RA No. 9184 in the conduct of public bidding to avoid suspension/disallowance in audit.

It is recommended that the management submit an explanation for the non-creation of the LCPC.  It is further recommended that management create a Local Council for the Protection of Children which could directly cater the needs of the supposed beneficiaries.  Moreover, it is recommended that management appropriate 1% of its IRA for LCPC related programs, activities and projects.

Require the Municipal Accountant to established RPT and SET Receivables for the current year based on the report of the Municipal Treasurer showing the list of the taxpayers and the amount due to accurately present the RPT/SET Receivable accounts in the financial statements and to adhere to the afore-stated provisions of the NGAS Manual in the setting up of RPT/SET Receivables for the year.  Proper recording of RPT/SET collections should also be observed to present an accurate financial statement for the year.

observations.recommendation:
